title: planmemreplay
description: This MEG dataset contains recordings from a study investigating planning, memory, and replay mechanisms in human cognition. The dataset includes magnetoencephalography recordings with synchronized trigger channels for precise event marking, along with detailed event timing information across multiple experimental phases (localizer, structure and reward learning, and post-learning). Photodiode-calibrated timing information indicates approximately 20 ms delay between trigger signals and visual stimulus presentation.
methods_description: Data were collected using magnetoencephalography (MEG) with integrated trigger channels for event marking. Visual stimuli were presented via projector with timing calibrated using photodiode measurements, revealing approximately 20 ms delay between trigger onset and visual stimulus presentation. Event timing information is provided in separate event files organized by participant.

funding_references.funder_name: R.D. is supported by Wellcome Trust Investigator Award 098362/ Z/12/Z.
The Max Planck University College London Centre is a joint initiative supported by University College London and the Max Planck Society.
